title: The Preemie-Test for Assessment of Newborn Skin Maturity
author: Zilma Reis
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7ynhpve

steps.title: The foot of the newborn examination
steps.instruction: The foot of the newborn examination: Expose the newborn's foot gently. Do not raise the lower limb to perform the exam. Do not handle the newborn. Instead, examine the child where he is being cared for: inside the incubator, warm crib or mother's lap.

steps.title: Determining the position of the skin assessment
steps.instruction: Determining the position of the skin assessment: The Preemie-Test should be performed on the skin of the posterior portion of the sole of the foot (Figure 1). Imagine two diagonal lines running through the child's sole region and choose the central area where these lines meet. Figure 1 - Standard position to touch the newborn's skin with the sensor of the device

steps.title: Position of the sensor during the assessment
steps.instruction: Position of the sensor during the assessment: Touch the skin with the sensor should perpendicularly (90 degrees - Figure 2) Figure 2 - Axis of the sensor when touching the skin

steps.title: Performing the test
steps.instruction: Performing the test: Follow the instructions according to the messages that will appear on the Preemie-Test display. Repeat the assessment three times in the same site of the skin foot.
